general_info.definition: Fluorescent carbon quantum dots synthesized from plant(Prosopis juliflora) leaves via used as an 'off-on' fluorescence probe for mercury and chemet sensing.

method.conditions: Prosopis juliflora leaves were washed with water, then heated at 200°C for about 30min in a crucible

post_treatment: Dispersion  in 10mL acetic acid (50% V/V) or 10mL ethanol for 24h

optical.emission_max: 396nm at excitation of 325nm and 437nm at excitation of 350nm

structural.crystallinity: presence of intense peak at 2Ɵ=22.12 and weak peak at 2Ɵ=42.42 are related to the plane of (533) and (101) diffraction pattern of plane of graphitic carbon

applications.performance: Linear range: 5-500 ng/mL-1; Limit of detection(LOD) based on 3Sb was 1.26 ng/mL-1 (or 6.3 nM); Relative standard deviation: 4.5% at 50 ng/mL.
applications.comparative_advantage: Green synthesis, low-cost, high selectivity. Dual sensing capability (Hg(II) and chemet) with one probe.

applications.performance: Linear range: 2.5-22.5 ng/mL-1;  Limit of detection(LOD) based on 3Sb: 1.4 ng/mL; RSD: 1.3% at 10 ng/mL-1.
applications.comparative_advantage: Dual sensing capability (Hg(II) and chemet) with one probe. Useful for monitoring chelation therapy in mercury poisoning.
